apt: adjective. Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.

pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).

ras: noun. An Ethiopian king or prince.

rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.

rots: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rot
sap: noun. The juice of plants of any kind, especially the ascending and descending juices or circulating fluid essential to nutrition.

star: noun. Any small, natural and bright dot in the sky, most visible in the night or twilight sky.

stop: noun. ITU & IMO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for full stop / period.
strap: noun. A long, narrow, pliable strip of leather, cloth, or the like.

appro: noun. The situation where goods, especially jewellery, must be returned by the retailer if not successfully sold.
asport: noun. An object that is spontaneously moved or "teleported" to another place.
parp: interjection. Representing the sound of a car horn, a trumpet, or breaking wind.
pato: noun. The national sport of Argentina, a game played on horseback that combines elements of polo and basketball.
poa: noun. Any grass of the genus Poa, especially Poa annua.
